# `simple_modal`

A block tag that renders a Bootstrap 5 modal **without** a trigger button. Use this when you want to wire up your own trigger element via `data-bs-target`.

Requires **Bootstrap JS** (`bootstrap.bundle.min.js`), included in the library's base template.

## Load

```django
{% load sample_tags %}
```

## Signature

```
{% simple_modal [id_modal] [title] [size] %}
    ...modal body content...
{% endsimple_modal %}
```

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `id_modal` | `str` | Auto-generated | HTML `id` for the modal element. Use this to wire up external triggers. Auto-generated with `"modal-"` prefix if omitted. |
| `title` | `str` | `"Modal Title"` | Title displayed in the modal header. |
| `size` | `str` | `None` | Bootstrap modal size modifier: `"sm"`, `"lg"`, or `"xl"`. Omitted if not set. |

## Examples

```django
{# Modal with a custom trigger button in the same template #}
<button data-bs-toggle="modal" data-bs-target="#my-modal" class="btn btn-primary">Open</button>
{% simple_modal id_modal="my-modal" title="Details" %}
    <p>Modal body content.</p>
{% endsimple_modal %}

{# Large modal #}
{% simple_modal id_modal="report-modal" title="Report" size="lg" %}
    <p>Wide modal content.</p>
{% endsimple_modal %}

{# Auto-generated id — use a template variable to capture it #}
{% simple_modal title="Info" %}
    <p>Content here.</p>
{% endsimple_modal %}
```

## Comparison with `simple_popup`

| | `simple_modal` | `simple_popup` |
|---|---|---|
| Trigger button | Not rendered — provide your own | Rendered automatically |
| When to use | Custom trigger UI, multiple triggers for the same modal | Standalone popup with its own button |

## Notes

- Use `simple_modal` when you need full control over the trigger (e.g. a table row, an icon button, or a trigger generated by JavaScript).
- Use [`simple_popup`](simple_popup.md) when a standard button trigger alongside the modal is sufficient.
